title: Tmux & Sesh
text:
Terminal multiplexer for managing multiple terminal sessions.



Ctrl+b + b: Open Sesh session picker
Ctrl+b + c: Create new window
Ctrl+b + %: Split pane vertically
Ctrl+b + ": Split pane horizontally

title: Tmux & Sesh
text:
Sesh
Session manager for Tmux with pre-configured sessions.

Pre-configured Sessions

Downloads: File management with Yazi
Dotfiles: Config editing with Neovim
Data730: Jupyter notebooks
Blog: Blog writing environment

More documentation coming soon…

title: Getting Started
text:
GNU Stow: For symlink management
Git: For version control
Neovim (>= 0.9.0): For LazyVim
Tmux: Terminal multiplexer
Wezterm: Terminal emulator

title: Getting Started
text:
GNU Stow: For symlink management
Git: For version control
Neovim (>= 0.9.0): For LazyVim
Tmux: Terminal multiplexer
Wezterm: Terminal emulator

title: Getting Started
text:
Installation

1. Clone the Repository
git clone https://github.com/chuckpr/dotfiles.git ~/dotfiles
cd ~/dotfiles


2. Deploy Configurations
Use GNU Stow to create symlinks:
# Deploy specific configurations
stow lazyvim    # → ~/.config/lazyvim
stow tmux       # → ~/.config/tmux
stow wezterm    # → ~/.config/wezterm
stow starship   # → ~/.config/starship
stow sesh       # → ~/.config/sesh

# Or deploy all at once
stow */


3. Install Plugin Managers
Tmux Plugin Manager (TPM):
git clone https://github.com/tmux-plugins/tpm ~/.tmux/plugins/tpm
Then inside tmux: Ctrl+b + I to install plugins.
LazyVim Plugins: Open Neovim and LazyVim will automatically install plugins. Because the config lives in ~/.config/lazyvim/ (not the default ~/.config/nvim/), you must set NVIM_APPNAME:
NVIM_APPNAME=lazyvim nvim
Add this to your fish config so it applies permanently:
set -gx NVIM_APPNAME lazyvim

title: Getting Started
text:
Configuration

Atuin (Special Case)
Atuin is not managed by Stow. Its configuration lives at:
~/.local/share/atuin/config.toml
Manually copy or symlink if needed.

title: Getting Started
text:
Verification
Test your setup:
# Check Neovim
NVIM_APPNAME=lazyvim nvim --version

# Check Tmux
tmux -V

# Start a Sesh session
sesh connect dotfiles






Troubleshooting



If symlinks aren’t created, verify your .stowrc is configured correctly:
cat ~/.stowrc

title: Terminal Configuration
text:
Starship
Fast, customizable shell prompt with: - Python environment indicators - AWS profile display - Battery status - Git status
More documentation coming soon…

title: LazyVim Configuration
text: LazyVim is a Neovim configuration framework that provides a modern IDE experience. The config is deployed to ~/.config/lazyvim/ via Stow, so Neovim must be launched with NVIM_APPNAME=lazyvim (set in fish config) to pick it up instead of the default ~/.config/nvim/.

title: LazyVim Configuration
text: LazyVim is a Neovim configuration framework that provides a modern IDE experience. The config is deployed to ~/.config/lazyvim/ via Stow, so Neovim must be launched with NVIM_APPNAME=lazyvim (set in fish config) to pick it up instead of the default ~/.config/nvim/.

title: LazyVim Configuration
text:
Plugin Management
Plugins are managed via Lazy.nvim and organized in lua/plugins/.

Key Plugins

CodeCompanion: AI integration with Claude Opus 4.5
Which-key: Helix-style keymaps
Render Markdown: Enhanced markdown rendering
Jupytext: Edit Jupyter notebooks as .py files

title: LazyVim Configuration
text:
Usage
# Inside Neovim
:Lazy                 # Open plugin manager
:Lazy sync            # Update plugins
:LazyExtras           # Browse available extras
More documentation coming soon…

title: Welcome to Dotfiles Documentation
text:
This is the documentation site for my personal dotfiles repository.


This repository contains my carefully curated development environment configurations:

LazyVim: Neovim configuration with 150+ plugins and AI integration
Tmux + Sesh: Terminal multiplexing with session management
Wezterm: Modern GPU-accelerated terminal emulator
Starship: Fast, customizable shell prompt
Atuin: Encrypted command history with sync




# Clone the repository
git clone https://github.com/chuckpr/dotfiles.git
cd dotfiles

# Deploy configurations with GNU Stow
stow lazyvim    # requires NVIM_APPNAME=lazyvim in fish config
stow tmux
stow wezterm
stow starship
stow sesh



Everything is themed with Gruvbox Dark for a consistent visual experience across all tools.






Note



This site is built with Quarto and deployed automatically via GitHub Actions.






Learn more in Getting Started
Explore the configuration sections in the sidebar
